{{Quote|We can safely ignore these contemptible little fur-balls.|Captain Toss, on the Ewoks|<ref name="ISP" />}}
'''Toss''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] who served as the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of [[IX3244-B]], a [[Survey Team]] tasked with finding a construction site for the Galactic Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. During [[Mission 759/B]], the team discovered the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], which Toss viewed as a good [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] construction site. When [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kiviett]], Toss's second-in-command, reported the dangers of Endor's native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], Toss dismissed the latter's concerns. However, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] would later commission a second Survey Team to investigate Endor upon being displeased by Toss's superficial reports.

==Biography==
===Surveying Endor===
{{Quote|He [Lord Vader] was confident that no indigenous lifeform could pose a threat to vastly superior Imperial forces. My commanding officer, Captain Toss, agreed.|Kiviett describes Toss|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:Moon_of_Endor_NEGAS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Forest Moon of Endor, which Toss and Survey Team IX3244-B discovered during Mission 759/B]]
Toss was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] who attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]].<ref name="ISP" /> At a certain [[Time/Legends|time]] following the [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] decreed the construction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. Thousands of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Survey Team]]s searched [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for a place to construct the [[Space station/Legends|station]].<ref name="ISP" /> For [[Mission 759/B]],<ref name="SWS">{{WEGCite|story=Report to Imperial Command|book=The Star Wars Sourcebook|reprint=1}}</ref> Captain Toss was the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of Survey Team [[IX3244-B]], with [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kiviett]] as his second-in-command. Including Kiviett, the team consisted of at least two [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and at least three men in light [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="ISP" />

Toss and his team visited several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] before their survey [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] found the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. [[Orbit/Legends|Orbital]] scans revealed that Endor was capable of sustaining [[Human/Legends|Human]] life. [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] viewed the remote [[Moon/Legends|moon]] as a good location for a construction site, also viewing Endor's indigenous life forms as posing no threat to the Empire's presence, with Toss agreeing.<ref name="ISP" />

===Mission 759/B and later Imperial defeat===
{{Quote|The blind fool was pleased. Endor's moon would be perfect. Surely I wasn't put off by a few natives with drums?|Kiviett describes Toss dismissing his concerns about Endor|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:VictoryOnEndor.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Ewoks, whose threat to the Empire was dismissed by Toss]]
When scans showed many [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] and primitive life forms on Endor, Toss ordered Kiviett and others to go to the moon and investigate as he stayed on the frigate to make a [[holoreport]] on the moon's discovery. Upon Kiviett's return to the frigate, he reported to Toss that Endor's [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] led them in circles and prevented them from reaching the [[Species/Legends|species']]<ref name="ISP" /> [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]].<ref name="FF">{{FactFile|11|END 7-10|The Battle of Endor}}</ref> However, Toss viewed the Ewoks as no threat and also ignored Kiviett's suggestion to apply [[Forest/Legends|forest]] camouflage to all the Imperial vehicles and armor.<ref name="ISP" /> Toss would report that Mission 759/B was a success<ref name="SWS" /> in discovering Endor as a location for the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] construction.<ref name="ISP" />

Ultimately, Palpatine was displeased with IX3244-B's superficial reports and sent a second Survey Team led by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Pfilbee Jhorn]] to Endor. Jhorn was also displeased with the report, and the failure to mention the presence of such dangers as [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]], [[Condor dragon/Legends|condor dragons]], and [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] Marauders made Jhorn view IX3244-B as disgraces to the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]].<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="FF" /> the remote moon's native Ewoks helped the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] defeat the Empire in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and a [[Prisoner/Legends|captive]] Kiviett reported his experience with IX3244-B to Alliance [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]].<ref name="ISP" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Their technology is laughably primitive.|Captain Toss, dismissing the Ewoks' potential danger in his holoreport|<ref name="ISP" />}}
[[File:PfilbeeJhorn2-ISWUp114.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sergeant Pfilbee Jhorn (pictured) was displeased with Toss's superficial holoreport.]]
Toss considered the Ewoks' primitive [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] to be no match for stormtroopers and also viewed the remote location as perfectly meeting Vader's requirements. He further laughed at Kiviett's reported trouble with the Ewoks, believing them to be no threat and saying [[Death/Legends|exterminating]] the species could be easily done if they proved to be too much trouble. Toss also agreed with Darth Vader that an environment already capable of supporting Human life was better than wasting time on constructing [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] systems and domes for the Death Star [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator]].<ref name="ISP" />

In Kiviett's mind, Toss ignored the former's report on the Ewoks being a threat, as he was in a rush to be credited with establishing the new Death Star's construction site. He likewise also ignored Kiviett's suggestion for forest camouflage added to the Imperial vehicles and armor on Endor.<ref name="ISP" /> Correspondingly, Palpatine was not satisfied with Toss's rushed report, and Jhorn felt no differently during his assignment leading the second Survey Team.<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe" />

==Behind the scenes==
Toss was created for "[[Report to Imperial Command]]," a short narrative story included in [[1987]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Curtis Smith]]. As the [[in-universe]] author of a report on IX3244-B's survey of Endor,<ref name="SWS" /> the character was later named in "[[Imperial Scouting Party]]," a story released in [[1990]] for [[Michael Stern]]'s ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''.<ref name="ISP" />
